violations.text: Stainer observed in one of the hand-washing sinks. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/24/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/24/2025
Spray bottles used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/24/2025
Containers of sauces were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/24/2025

The cutting board of the large prep unit is very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 12/08/2025
The condensate from the walk-in cooler is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 11/24/2025
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Utensils must be stored in a clean, dry location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. Test strips must be obtained. New Violation. Correct By: 12/24/2024
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dishwasher. Thermo labels/device must be obtained. New Violation. Correct By: 12/24/2024

The condensate from the walk-in cooler observed to be draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain or evaporator New Violation. Correct By: 12/24/2024
The floor and wall junctures in the bar do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. New Violation. Correct By: 12/24/2024
